title: Nvidia and IREN unveil 5-gigawatt AI infrastructure pact tied to $2.1B share option
subtitle: Former bitcoin miner IREN gets a Nvidia equity option and a $3.4B GPU services contract; the deal caps a week in which Nvidia's 2026 equity commitments crossed $40 billion.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: On May 7, Nvidia and Australian-listed data-center operator IREN announced a strategic partnership to deploy up to 5 gigawatts of Nvidia DSX-aligned AI infrastructure across IREN's pipeline, with IREN's 2-gigawatt Sweetwater campus in Texas as the flagship site. IREN granted Nvidia a five-year right to buy up to 30 million shares at $70 per share — a $2.1 billion equity option. Separately, IREN signed a five-year, $3.4 billion contract to deliver managed GPU cloud services to Nvidia for its internal AI and research workloads.

The deal is the latest in a now-recurring pattern: Nvidia takes equity in a partner who, in turn, commits to buying or hosting Nvidia hardware. CNBC and TechCrunch reported May 9 that Nvidia's 2026 equity commitments now exceed $40 billion across roughly two dozen deals — anchored by a $30 billion stake in OpenAI and including $3.2 billion into Corning for optical fiber and the $2.1 billion IREN option. Wedbush analyst Matthew Bryson called the structure a 'circular investment theme' but argued it could function as a competitive moat if the bets land.

IREN's trajectory is itself the story. The company spent its first decade as a bitcoin miner; the partnership reframes it as an AI hyperscaler-in-the-making, deploying Nvidia's DSX architecture rather than mining ASICs. Power-rich data-center operators — bitcoin miners, crypto co-locators, even nuclear-adjacent campuses — are being absorbed into the AI compute supply chain because the fastest-to-build capacity in 2026 is whatever already has grid interconnects.

For learners: when one company invests in another that buys its products, ask whether the underlying demand would exist without the investment. With Nvidia's equity portfolio, the answer is mixed — some partners (OpenAI, Anthropic) clearly want the chips regardless; others would not have hit the same scale this fast without the capital. Following the dollars and the gigawatts both tells you which firms are real infrastructure and which are accounting bets dressed up as partnerships.

sources.title: NVIDIA and IREN Announce Strategic Partnership to Accelerate Deployment of up to 5 Gigawatts of AI Infrastructure — NVIDIA Newsroom
